The documented 6 cities + ManagerLocation class + general ASCII case are fully fixed; the accented-place residual is filed separately rather than blocking closure of the documented artifacts.

NF-final-resid-002.title: Cover Letter Studio silently drops the honest async no-résumé refusal

evidence.alert_shown_both_iterations: F2 (no résumé) clicked 'Generate Draft' in Cover Letter Studio -> a role=alert element rendered the exact honest message 'Add your resume before generating a cover letter.' BOTH iterations (VERIFIED TWICE, real browser).
evidence.no_silent_noop: letter cards created = 0 (no letter); alert present and non-empty; NOT a silent no-op.
evidence.quota_not_charged: F2 coverLetter runs (x5) all costUsd=0/status=failed with honest error; net runsUsed=1 is the emailAgent TRIAGE (legit work), NOT any résumé-refusal (db/quota-and-leakscan.json).
evidence.regenerate_path: UNREACHABLE for a no-résumé account (no letter cards exist to regenerate; rail Regenerate/Request Changes need a selected letter) — documented as N/A, not a gap.

new_findings.title: JD Keyword Coverage still leaks a CamelCase concatenation artifact for ACCENTED/non-ASCII place names glued to a label ('MünchenLocation' -> chip 'nchenLocation')
new_findings.class: narrow residual of NF-final-resid-001 / NF-final-PII-001 / MV-cover-letter-studio-006 (accented-proper-noun case)
new_findings.epistemic: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] leak observed; [INFERRED] root cause from code reading
new_findings.expected: No concatenation artifact surfaces as a keyword regardless of which (incl. accented/unicode) proper noun is glued to the label; legit mixed-case tech preserved.
new_findings.observed: A scraped JD gluing an accented city to a label ('MünchenLocation') surfaces the fragment 'nchenLocation' as a top keyword chip. Reproduced 3x: API run1, API run2 (fresh session), real-browser panel (card2, Novatlas Global). Companion accented gluings whose label stayed ASCII-attached (ZuerichSalary/TokyoSalary/SãoPauloCompensation) were correctly dropped; the leak is specific to the non-ASCII char SEVERING the token.
new_findings.root_cause_inferred: The tokenizer _WORD_RE = [A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9+#./-]* is ASCII-only, so 'MünchenLocation' splits at 'ü' into 'M' + 'nchenLocation'. For 'nchenLocation', _CAMEL_HUMP_RE.findall yields only ['Location'] (the lowercase fragment 'nchen' starts no CamelCase segment), so len(segments) < 2 early-returns False in _is_camel_concatenation_artifact BEFORE the new any-segment-in-_ARTIFACT_LABEL_WORDS check runs. 'nchenLocation' then survives _is_semantic_keyword and scores +45 internal-uppercase in _skill_score.
new_findings.repro: As a Pro user create a Job whose scraped-style description contains 'München' glued to a label with no space (e.g. 'MünchenLocation: hybrid'); generate a cover letter; GET /cover-letters/{id}/insights -> keywords.items contains 'nchenLocation'.
new_findings.fix_hint: Either (a) also flag a single-segment token whose ONE CamelCase segment is a label word preceded by a lowercase fragment (drop the len(segments)>=2 gate for the label-word branch), or (b) normalize/transliterate non-ASCII letters before tokenizing so 'München'+label splits cleanly and hits the existing label rule.
new_findings.materiality: Narrow: requires an accented place name directly glued to a label word in a scraped JD; degrades one keyword chip on the paid intelligence rail (no PII/security). Filed to keep the fixers honest against the finding's own 'regardless of which proper noun' criterion — the brief explicitly asked me to probe unicode place names.

new_findings.title: Agents console (and pipeline 'Run All') mis-surface an honest async no-résumé refusal as a GREEN FALSE SUCCESS; fitScorer surfaces a MISLEADING remediation — same bug class as NF-final-resid-002, on the agents/pipeline surface the fixer flagged out-of-scope
new_findings.class: LEAD-CHASE outcome — adjacent to NF-final-resid-002 (backend refusal is honest; the frontend surfacing on dashboard/agents mis-labels it)
new_findings.epistemic: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] UI + backend both from THIS run
new_findings.screen: dashboard/agents (per-agent Run buttons + 'Run All' pipeline); apps/web/src/app/dashboard/agents/page.tsx via lib/agents-feedback.ts

backend_truth.tailor: POST /agents/tailor/run (F2, no résumé) -> 202 -> BackgroundJob status='completed', result.missingResume=true, message='Add your resume before tailoring or generating an application.'
backend_truth.coverLetter: completed, missingResume=true, message='Add your resume before generating a cover letter.'
backend_truth.pipeline: completed, missingResume=true, message='Add your resume before scoring jobs against it.'
backend_truth.fitScorer: sync 422 {detail:'Add your resume before scoring jobs against it.'}
backend_truth.leak_scan: NO 'MissingResumeError'/'Traceback'/'resume_grounding' in any AgentRun.error/BackgroundJob.result/error (honest message only). quota charged 0 on every résumé-refusal (db/quota-and-leakscan.json).

ui_observed_prod_browser.resumeTailoring(tailor): GREEN success notice: 'Tailor finished — resume tailored with 0 accepted changes. review the diff in Resume Studio →' (FALSE SUCCESS — nothing tailored, no résumé; CTA to a non-existent diff).
ui_observed_prod_browser.coverLetter: GREEN success notice: 'CoverLetter finished — a draft is awaiting your sign-off. review it in Approvals →' (FALSE SUCCESS — no draft; CTA to empty Approvals).
ui_observed_prod_browser.run_all(pipeline): GREEN success notice: 'Pipeline complete — no jobs matched yet. Run Scout with a different query, or browse what was discovered: open Jobs →' (FALSE SUCCESS — the honest 'Add your resume' refusal dropped).
ui_observed_prod_browser.atsOptimization/matchScoring/skillGap(fi: RED error notice: 'fitScorer needs more data first — run Scout to discover jobs, then try again. open Jobs →' (MISLEADING — drops the honest 'Add your resume' detail; wrong remediation since jobs already exist). At least it is an error, not a false success.
ui_observed_prod_browser.emailAgent: GREEN 'emailAgent finished successfully.' — ACCEPTABLE (triage mode needs no résumé; genuine completion, charged 0.0006 legit work).

new_findings.root_cause_inferred: resolveRun() returns job.result on status='completed', so a missingResume-completed refusal reaches agentSuccessNotice(backend, output) / pipelineCompletionNotice(output), which read output.changes/scored/hasLetter/approvalRequired (all undefined for a refusal) and fabricate a success string — they never check output.missingResume/output.message. fitScorer's sync 422 hits runErrorNotice(e,'fitScorer') whose status===422 branch returns a hardcoded 'run Scout to discover jobs' message, ignoring the honest 422 detail. This is the SAME class the fixer explicitly flagged as out-of-scope for NF-final-resid-002 (fix-log.md §2.2/§5: 'dashboard/agents/page.tsx and email/page.tsx use the identical resolveRun()/pollJob() mechanism ... whether those pages have the identical frontend surfacing gap was NOT investigated ... flagging for the orchestrator').
new_findings.repro: Log in as a Pro user with NO résumé; dashboard/agents; click Run on Resume Tailoring or Cover Letter (or 'Run All') -> GREEN false-success notice instead of the honest 'Add your resume…' message. Click Run on ATS Optimization/Match Scoring/Skill Gap -> misleading 'run Scout' error.
new_findings.fix_hint: In agents/page.tsx trigger() (and runPipeline()): before agentSuccessNotice/pipelineCompletionNotice, check `output.missingResume || (typeof output.message === 'string' && !output.<successKey>)` and surface output.message as an info/error notice (mirror NF-final-resid-002's applyCoverLetterResult). Make runErrorNotice surface the real 422 detail (extractApiDetail) for the agent-run flow instead of a hardcoded 'run Scout' line. Apply the same guard to email/page.tsx per the fixer's own escalation.
evidence: api/test3-leadchase-api.json (backend truth, all 4 agents + pipeline + quota)

sec7_spot_check.verdict: PASS for the production api (0 REPRODUCIBLE 5xx); shared-log 500 anomaly adjudicated to a concurrent/stale-code process
sec7_spot_check.console: pageerrors=0 on every page exercised (test1/test2/test3/pipeline). The single console 'error' in the lead-chase run is the fitScorer honest 422 network response (a legitimate client-error the UI surfaces via a notice), not an uncaught JS error.
sec7_spot_check.server_5xx: 27 untimestamped '500 Internal Server Error' lines exist in the shared /var/log/aether/api.log during the window, from a SEPARATE process running stale/broken code (import-time 'cannot import name HTTPException from starlette.exceptions' + a pre-MV-agents-002 'GET /agents/runs?limit=-5' -> InvalidRowCountInLimitClause 500). NOT the production api: (1) current prod code has the ge=0 guard and returns 422 for ?limit=-5 LIVE; (2) every endpoint that 500'd reprobes clean (200/401) now; (3) the 500 lines carry NO ISO timestamp (running uvicorn's log-config prefixes every line); (4) NRestarts=0 — prod api never crashed/restarted during the window. All of MY requests + fresh reprobes returned correct codes. Matches the brief's note that a concurrent fixer/pytest may be active. See console/server-log-window-adjudication.txt.
sec7_spot_check.no_class_leak: No 'MissingResumeError'/'Traceback'/'resume_grounding' in any user-visible payload or refusal row (backend refusals carry only the honest message).

lead_chase_outcome: NEW FINDING NF-final-closure-002 (MEDIUM). The fixer's flagged lead is REAL: dashboard/agents (and the pipeline 'Run All') share resolveRun()/pollJob() and DO have the same surfacing gap NF-final-resid-002 fixed for the Cover Letter Studio — but WORSE, because agentSuccessNotice/pipelineCompletionNotice render a GREEN FALSE SUCCESS (tailor: 'resume tailored with 0 accepted changes'; coverLetter: 'a draft is awaiting your sign-off'; pipeline: 'Pipeline complete') with dead-end CTAs, dropping the honest 'Add your resume…' message; fitScorer surfaces a misleading 'run Scout' remediation. Backend is honest (missingResume+message, 0 charge, no class leak) — this is purely a frontend surfacing defect on the agents/pipeline (and by inference email) pages. The email page (dashboard/email) was not directly driven this run but shares the identical mechanism and is flagged for the same fix.
